Loud noise gives me a headache.
Rest will help your headache.
quizzes: I have a terrible __________ today, I think it is because the sun was very bright earlier.
All that loud music and shouting gave me a really bad __________ during the party.
If you have a __________, you should try to lie down in a quiet and dark room for a while.

vocabularies.definition: The state of being well and free from illness.
vocabularies.example:
Eat fruit for good health.
Exercise is good for health.
A health check-up.
quizzes: It is very important to eat plenty of fresh fruit and vegetables for your good __________.
Regular exercise like running or swimming is very good for your physical and mental __________.
Our teacher talked to us about the importance of good __________ and getting enough sleep.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people who are ill or injured are treated by doctors and nurses.
vocabularies.example:
He works in a hospital.
She was taken to the hospital.
A children's hospital.
quizzes: My aunt works as a nurse in a very large __________ in the center of the city.
The ambulance took the injured man to the nearest __________ for immediate treatment.
We visited our grandmother in the __________ yesterday and brought her some beautiful flowers.

vocabularies.definition: To cause physical pain to someone or yourself.
vocabularies.example:
Does it hurt?
I hurt my leg.
My throat hurts.
quizzes: Does your arm still __________ where you fell over on the playground this morning?
I __________ my left leg while I was playing football and now I can't walk very well.
My throat really __________ when I try to swallow some food or even a drink of water.

vocabularies.definition: The joint between the top and bottom parts of the leg.
vocabularies.example:
He fell and hurt his knee.
Bend your knees.
She's sitting on her mother's knee.
quizzes: I have a large purple bruise on my __________ because I fell off my bicycle yesterday.
Please bend your __________ until they almost touch the floor during our gym exercise.
The little boy was sitting happily on his mother's __________ while she read him a story.

vocabularies.definition: A substance used for treating illness or pain.
vocabularies.example:
Take your medicine.
A bottle of cough medicine.
The medicine tastes bad.
quizzes: Please remember to take your pink __________ three times a day after you eat your meals.
This bottle of cough __________ should help you feel much better and stop your coughing.
I know that the __________ tastes quite bad, but it will help you to get well more quickly.

vocabularies.definition: The part of the body that joins the head to the rest of the body.
vocabularies.example:
Wear a scarf around your neck.
He has a long neck.
She's wearing a gold necklace around her neck.
quizzes: You should wear a warm woolly scarf around your __________ to stay cozy in the cold winter air.
Giraffes are famous for having very long __________ that help them reach the leaves at the top of trees.
She is wearing a beautiful gold necklace around her __________ that her grandmother gave her.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to take care of sick or injured people, usually in a hospital.
vocabularies.example:
The nurse gave him an injection.
He's a school nurse.
She's a very kind nurse.
quizzes: The __________ was very kind and gentle when she was cleaning the cut on my knee.
My mother works as a __________ in the local hospital and she helps many people get better.
The school __________ checked all the children's heights and weights during the health week.

vocabularies.definition: One of the two parts of the body between the neck and the top of the arms.
vocabularies.example:
Carry the bag on your shoulder.
He hurt his shoulder.
She touched my shoulder.
quizzes: It is easier to carry that heavy school bag if you put it on your __________ using both straps.
I think I have pulled a muscle in my __________ while I was throwing the ball during the game.
The teacher gently touched my __________ and told me that I had done a very good job today.

vocabularies.definition: The part of the body where food goes when you eat it.
vocabularies.example:
My stomach is full.
He has a stomach-ache.
An empty stomach.
quizzes: Oh! I think I ate too much delicious cake and now my __________ is feeling very full.
I can hear your __________ growling! Are you feeling very hungry because you haven't eaten?
It is not a good idea to go for a long swim on a very full or a very empty __________.

vocabularies.definition: A pain in your stomach.
vocabularies.example:
I have a bad stomach-ache.
Too many sweets give me a stomach-ache.
Rest will help your stomach-ache.
quizzes: I have a very bad __________ because I think I ate some fruit that wasn't washed properly.
If you eat too many green apples, you might get a painful __________ later this afternoon.
The little boy was crying because he had a __________ and he just wanted to lie down.

vocabularies.definition: A small round piece of medicine; or a small computer.
vocabularies.example:
Take two tablets.
A vitamin tablet.
I play games on my tablet.
quizzes: The doctor told my father to take one small white __________ every morning for his health.
My sister loves playing educational games and watching cartoons on her new electronic __________.
I take a vitamin __________ every single day to help my body stay strong and healthy.

vocabularies.definition: How hot or cold something is; or when your body is too hot.
vocabularies.example:
What's the temperature outside?
She has a high temperature.
Check the temperature.
quizzes: The __________ outside is very low today, so please remember to wear your warm winter coat.
My mother used a thermometer to check my __________ and she said I have a slight fever.
What is the __________ of the water in the swimming pool? Is it warm enough for us to jump in?

vocabularies.definition: One of the five parts at the end of each foot.
vocabularies.example:
I hurt my big toe.
I have ten toes.
Touch your toes.
quizzes: I accidentally hit my big __________ on the corner of the heavy wooden bed and now it's sore.
Can you reach down and touch your __________ without bending your knees during our gym class?
Did you know that each of your feet has five __________ at the end, just like each of your hands has five fingers?

vocabularies.definition: The hard white objects in your mouth that you use for biting and chewing food.
vocabularies.example:
Brush your teeth.
A white tooth.
He lost a tooth.
quizzes: Please remember to brush your __________ carefully for two minutes every morning and every night.
My little brother is very excited because he just lost his first baby __________ today!
It is important to eat less sugar if you want to keep your __________ strong, white, and healthy.

vocabularies.definition: A pain in one of your teeth.
vocabularies.example:
I have a terrible toothache.
Go to the dentist if you have toothache.
Warm salt water helps toothache.
quizzes: I couldn't eat my crunchy apple today because I have a very painful __________ in a back tooth.
If you have a __________, you should visit the dentist as soon as possible to get it checked.
Sometimes, rinsing your mouth with warm salt water can help to make a __________ feel better.

vocabularies.definition: A person who performs in a play or film.
vocabularies.example:
He's a famous actor.

I want to be an actor.
quizzes: The main __________ in the movie was very talented and made the audience laugh a lot.
All the __________ in the school play had to learn many lines of dialogue by heart.
Would you like to be a famous movie __________ and travel to Hollywood one day?

vocabularies.definition: A person who creates art, especially paintings and drawings.
vocabularies.example:

The artist painted a portrait.
quizzes: The __________ used a large brush and many bright colors to create a beautiful painting of the sea.

We visited the studio of a famous local __________ and saw many of her amazing sculptures.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is trained to travel in a spacecraft.

A space astronaut.
quizzes: The brave __________ spent six months living and working on the International Space Station.
Do you really want to become an __________ and travel in a giant rocket to the moon one day?
The __________ wore a special white suit and a large helmet to protect himself in outer space.

vocabularies.definition: A person who works in business, especially one who has a high position in a company.
vocabularies.example:

A business meeting.
quizzes: My mother is a successful __________ who owns a small clothing shop in the city center.
The __________ wore a smart suit and carried a leather briefcase to his important meeting.
Being a __________ often means you have to travel a lot and meet many different people.

vocabularies.definition: A person who prepares food for eating; or to prepare food.
vocabularies.example:

quizzes: The __________ in the restaurant prepared a very delicious pasta dish for our dinner last night.

vocabularies.definition: A doctor who takes care of people's teeth.
vocabularies.example:

Visit the dentist regularly.
quizzes: I am going to visit the __________ this afternoon because I have a small hole in my tooth.
The __________ told me that I should brush my teeth twice a day and eat less candy.
It is important to visit your __________ regularly if you want to have a healthy and white smile.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to treat people who are sick or injured.
vocabularies.example:
The doctor is very kind.

A hospital doctor.
quizzes: The __________ used a stethoscope to listen to my heart and said that I was very healthy.

My cousin works as a __________ in a children's hospital and she enjoys helping them get well.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to design or build machines, engines, or bridges.

A bridge engineer.

vocabularies.definition: A person who owns or manages a farm.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to stop fires and rescue people.
vocabularies.example:

vocabularies.definition: A person who collects and writes news for newspapers, magazines, or television.
vocabularies.example:

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to repair machines, especially car engines.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to care for people who are sick or injured, especially in a hospital.

A hospital nurse.
She's a kind nurse.
quizzes: The __________ was very helpful and showed us where our grandfather's bed was in the hospital.

vocabularies.definition: A person who paints pictures or buildings.

vocabularies.definition: A person who takes photos especially as a job.

vocabularies.definition: A person who flies an aircraft.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is a member of the police.
vocabularies.example:

Call the police!

vocabularies.definition: A person who works in an office, writing letters and organizing meetings.

vocabularies.definition: A person who sings, especially as a job.

She's a pop singer.

vocabularies.definition: A person who works in a restaurant bringing food and drinks to customers.

A restaurant waiter.

vocabularies.definition: Used to say how far in the past something happened.

This beautiful old wooden bridge was built more than a __________ ago by local workers.

vocabularies.definition: At any time, especially used in questions and negative sentences.
vocabularies.example:

The best book ever.
Nothing ever happens.
quizzes: Have you __________ been to the top of a very high mountain or a tall building?
This is definitely the best chocolate cake that I have __________ tasted in my whole life!
Do you think that humans will __________ live on another planet like Mars in the future?

vocabularies.definition: Having come to an end; no longer doing something.

The game is finished.

vocabularies.definition: The time that will happen after the present.

quizzes: What do you think the schools of the __________ will be like in one hundred years' time?

vocabularies.definition: The study of past events, especially of a particular country or subject.

World history.
A history book.
I like history.
quizzes: My favorite subject at school is __________ because I enjoy learning about ancient civilizations.
We are reading a very interesting book about the __________ of our city this week.
The museum is full of many old objects that tell us a lot about the __________ of the world.

vocabularies.definition: The past tense of 'meet'.

quizzes: I __________ my best friend for the first time when we were both in the first grade at school.

vocabularies.definition: One single time; or at some time in the past.

I've been there once.
Once upon a time.
Do it once more.
quizzes: I have only been to that big water park __________, but I would really like to go again.
__________ upon a time, there was a brave knight who lived in a large stone castle.

vocabularies.definition: The time before the present; or further than a particular place.

In the past.

Are you still here?
It's still raining.
I still like it.
quizzes: Are you __________ working on that difficult math problem? You've been doing it for an hour!
It is __________ raining outside, so you should definitely take your umbrella with you.
I __________ have some of my favorite stickers that I collected when I was a little child.

Back then.
What happened then?

Back __________, we didn't have any computers or tablets to play with at home.

What are you doing tomorrow?
quizzes: I am going to the city museum with my whole class __________ morning for a school trip.

vocabularies.definition: The night of the present day.

Are you busy tonight?

quizzes: We are going to have a special dinner at a nice restaurant __________ to celebrate my birthday.

A centimeter is a small __________ of measurement that we use to find out how long things are.

vocabularies.definition: Up to the time that.

Until now.
Stay here until I come back.
quizzes: You should stay inside the classroom __________ the rain stops and the sun comes out again.
I didn't know how to play the guitar at all __________ my father started teaching me last month.
Please wait __________ I come back from the shop before you start eating the birthday cake.

vocabularies.definition: Used in negative sentences and questions to talk about something that has not happened before now.

I haven't seen it yet.
quizzes: I haven't finished my science project __________, but I hope to finish it by this evening.
Has the mailman arrived with our packages __________? I am waiting for an important letter.
No, I haven't seen the new movie about the space travelers __________, is it very exciting?
